text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared main repository. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a build, compiling the software to ensure it runs correctly. After the build, the system runs a series of automated tests to check for errors. This process helps catch issues early, before they reach production. By testing frequently, teams can identify and fix bugs quickly, saving time and reducing conflicts. CI pipelines are essential for smooth collaboration and reliable software delivery.
